topic: trying to meal prep halal meals for the week

turns.content: right I need to actually get my act together with food this week. can you help me come up with some halal meal prep ideas? I want to cook on Sunday and have food sorted for the whole work week

Absolutely! A few questions first so I can tailor the suggestions: What kind of protein do you usually go for — chicken, lamb, beef? And are you looking for stuff that reheats well for lunch at work, dinner at home, or both?

chicken mostly cos its cheapest. I do like lamb but the prices are mad right now. and yeah mainly lunches for work — I need something I can stick in a container and microwave. dinners I can usually throw something quick together when I get home

Makes sense. Here are some ideas that are all chicken-based, reheat well, and can be made in bulk on Sunday:

1. **Chicken and rice bowls** — marinate chicken thighs in suqaar-style spices (cumin, coriander, turmeric, bit of chilli), bake them off, serve with basmati rice and some roasted veg. Classic and easy.

2. **Chicken pasta bake** — penne with a tomato-based sauce, shredded chicken, spinach. Portion into containers. Reheats perfectly.

3. **Chicken wraps/shawarma style** — cook up shawarma-spiced chicken, make a garlic yoghurt sauce, portion the chicken and sauce separately, assemble with salad each morning.

4. **Chicken curry** — big batch of a simple curry (coconut milk base or tomato base), portion with rice.

Which of those sounds appealing?

omg the shawarma one sounds unreal but I feel like it would go soggy if I pre-make the wraps? and the chicken curry one is deffo doable. my mum makes incredible suqaar but I've never actually tried making it myself loool

You're right about the wraps — don't assemble them in advance. Keep the chicken, sauce, and any salad separate in the container. Bring a wrap or flatbread on the side and put it together at work. That way nothing goes soggy.

For the suqaar, it's actually really simple if you've watched your mum make it. The base is usually: oil, onions, garlic, cumin, coriander, turmeric, and then diced chicken (or beef traditionally). Cook it down, add a bit of tomato paste, maybe some peppers. Serve over rice or with flatbread. You could make a massive batch and it keeps well for 4-5 days in the fridge.

okay wait so if I did the suqaar on Sunday and the curry on Sunday I'd basically have two options for the week? like Monday suqaar, Tuesday curry, Wednesday suqaar etc so I dont get bored of eating the same thing every day

Exactly — alternating between two meals means you're not sick of either by Wednesday. Make a big pot of each, portion out into 5 containers (3 of one, 2 of the other, or however you like), and you're set. Cook one big batch of rice that goes with both.

The total cook time on Sunday would probably be about 90 minutes for both, including rice. Not bad for a whole week of lunches.
90 minutes is very doable. honestly the amount I spend on meal deals and random food from Pret is actually criminal. I worked it out once and it was like £40-50 a week on lunches alone

That's over £200 a month just on work lunches. A big shop for meal prep chicken, rice, spices, veg — you're probably looking at £15-20 for the whole week. So you'd save over £150 a month easily, plus you'd actually be eating proper home-cooked food instead of whatever Pret has left at 2pm.

dont even get me started on Pret at 2pm. by that time all the good stuff is gone and you're left with like an egg mayo sandwich and regret. right I'm making a shopping list. chicken thighs, basmati rice, onions, garlic, tinned tomatoes, coconut milk, spices. what else?

Good list so far. I'd add:

- Tomato paste (for the suqaar)
- Peppers — red and green work well in both dishes
- Fresh coriander for garnish (optional but nice)
- Yoghurt — works as a side with both and lasts the week
- Spinach — chuck a handful into the curry, adds nutrition without effort
- Some kind of hot sauce if you like heat (chilli flakes work too)
- Containers if you don't already have them — get the microwave-safe ones with lids from Asda or Home Bargains, they're cheap

I have containers from a previous meal prep attempt that lasted exactly one week loool. oh also — do you think the curry would freeze okay? like if I made a double batch could I freeze half for the week after?

Curry freezes brilliantly — it's one of the best things to batch freeze because the flavours actually develop more over time. Make a double batch, freeze half in individual portions, and you've got next week sorted too. Just defrost overnight in the fridge and reheat. Rice is a bit trickier to freeze safely — you want to cool it quickly and reheat it piping hot — but it's doable. Or just make fresh rice each Sunday since it only takes 15 minutes.

okay thats the plan then. double batch curry, freeze half. suqaar fresh for the week. I'm actually excited about this which is concerning because its literally just cooking loool. my mum would be proud if I can actually get the suqaar right. right I need to go to Asda before it shuts. thanks for the help!

Ha, getting excited about meal prep is a good sign — it means you'll actually do it! Go grab those ingredients and enjoy the cooking tomorrow. Two dishes, keep it simple, and you'll save yourself a fortune on Pret. Good luck with the suqaar!
